exemplar.title: How can the Pell-type equation and Vieta jumping method be applied to solve integer divisibility problems in the OSN 2023 District Level problem?

detailedExemplarData.title: How can the Pell-type equation and Vieta jumping method be applied to solve integer divisibility problems in the OSN 2023 District Level problem?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The title and abstract clearly communicate the scope and focus on Pell-type equations and Vieta jumping.
The research question is explicitly stated, well-focused, and remains central throughout the essay.
The Methodology section is clearly signposted and demonstrates effective planning and informed selection of two complementary analytical methods.
Section headings and the overall organization guide the reader through the argument coherently.
weaknesses: Minor language awkwardness and isolated digressions (textbook-style expositions and a personal anecdote) momentarily blur concision.
Background on Vieta jumping appears before its formal definition, disrupting narrative flow.
Several derivations are compressed and some graph-based demonstrations serve more illustration than rigorous proof.
Occasional meanders (e.g. in section 5.3) distract from the research focus and require refocusing citation clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments consistently placed this work in the top band (5â6) for each sub-strand, reflecting clear communication of topic and focus, a well-defined research question, and a complete methodology. Minor digressions, compressed derivations and occasional focus drift justify withholding the maximum. A score of 5 balances strong coherence and planning against these small lapses, and aligns with the official examinerâs overall mark distribution.

strengths: Advanced number-theoretic concepts (Pell equations, continued fractions, Vieta jumping, modular arithmetic) are deployed accurately and fluently.
Subject-specific terminology and notation are introduced, defined and used consistently throughout.
Key worked examples (IMO problem, divisibility proofs) demonstrate confident manipulation of concepts.
The Olympiad problem is stated precisely in mathematical terms, framing the investigation clearly.
weaknesses: Occasional algebraic slips and abrupt step jumps reduce precision in a few derivations.
Some reliance on graphical intuition in place of full proof detracts from rigor.
Minor typographical repetitions and mis-labelled variables (e.g. switching t/s).
Omissions such as noting that d must be nonsquare in Pellâs equation or elaborating on continued fraction periodicity limit completeness.
Prose definitions (e.g. Vieta jumping) would benefit from concise mathematical statements, and source attribution could be more explicit.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both sub-strands of Knowledge and Understanding were rated in the 5â6 band, reflecting excellent subject-specific knowledge and terminology use with only minor lapses. Awarding 5/6 recognizes consistently relevant application and mostly effective use of sources, while accounting for precision and attribution weaknesses.

strengths: Logical case distinctions and discriminant arguments lead to the correct maximal n=169, demonstrating substantial analytical depth.
Seven authoritative sources are judiciously selected and integrated into the argument, showing strong research relevance.
The interplay between Pell-type methods and Vieta jumping is explicitly articulated, evidencing critical application.
Derivations of key divisibility conditions and prime-constraint arguments are concise and insightful.
A reasoned comparison of techniques shows emerging evaluative thinking.
weaknesses: Several transitional steps (e.g. bounding arguments, Vieta minimization) are truncated, requiring the reader to infer missing details.
Discussion and evaluation remains somewhat general, without deep probing of limitations or potential errors.
Source diversity could be greater (e.g. inclusion of primary research papers) to strengthen critical cross-comparison.
Some methodological steps (e.g. root-flipping in Vieta jumping) are described vaguely rather than rigorously formalized.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand-level ratings fell into the 7â9 band for research quality (8) and analysis depth (7), and the 4â6 band for discussion and evaluation (5). Averaging these strand results, and in order to meet the fixed total of 21, yields a justified score of 7/12, reflecting strong research and analysis tempered by a less critical evaluation strand.

strengths: The essay follows a clear academic structure (title page, abstract, contents, numbered sections, conclusion, bibliography) that supports understanding.
Equations are well formatted in LaTeX, graphs are consistently styled and captioned, and headings follow a logical hierarchy.
Page numbering and a stated word count are present, meeting formal IB requirements.
The table of contents with numbered sections enhances navigability.
weaknesses: Alignment remains left-justified and table spacing is inconsistent.
Some figure captions omit axis labels, and a few captions are repeated beneath images.
The contents page lacks dynamic hyperlinks or bookmarks for efficient navigation.
Overly long subsections and occasional digressions could be streamlined for greater readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation strands were assessed at 4/4 for structure and 3/4 for layout and formatting, placing the work in the top band overall. Minor polish issues prevent a perfect flawless rating, but the structure and formatting are strong. Awarding 4/4 aligns with the official total and reflects a clear, coherent presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly quoted on the title page and in the introduction, remains the focal point of every major section, and is revisited in the conclusion.  The scopeârestricting consideration to one Olympiad problem under p < 200 and exploring two specific techniquesâis appropriately narrow.  A few passages (e.g. section 5.3) meander into lengthy justifications that momentarily blur the focus, so a high but not maximal mark is warranted.

The analysis proceeds through logical case distinctions, discriminant conditions and recursive argument, culminating in the correct maximal \(n=169\).  Most conclusions are justified, and the interplay between the two methods is explicitly articulated.  Nevertheless, several transitions (e.g. bounding arguments in 5.2.2, step from general Pell solution to integer feasibility) are truncated, leaving the reader to fill gaps.  These omissions limit the overall effectiveness, anchoring the strand at the lower end of the 7-9 band.

Specialised terminologyââfundamental solutionâ, âcontinued fraction expansionâ, âquadratic Diophantineâ, âdiscriminantâ, âgcdâ etc.âis introduced, defined and used correctly across the essay.  Symbols are generally consistent and notation such as â\(\pmod{}\)â and â\(\mid\)â is handled with competence.  Minor typographical repetitions and a few mis-labelled variables (e.g. switching t/s) prevent a perfect score but overall usage is accurate and sustained.

Graphs are consistently styled and captioned, equations are rendered in LaTeX and headings follow a logical hierarchy.  The document now includes page numbers and a stated word count.  However, alignment remains left-justified, tables show inconsistent spacing, and some figure captions are repeated verbatim beneath the images, detracting from polish.  Overall layout meets most, but not all, IB formal expectations, placing it safely in the 3-4 band but nearer the lower end.

Throughout, the student deploys advanced number-theoretic knowledgeâPell equations, continued fractions, Vieta jumping, modular arithmeticâaccurately and mostly fluently.  Worked examples, discriminant tests and recursive constructions illustrate confident manipulation of concepts.  Occasional algebraic slips (e.g. mis-stated inequalities, abrupt step jumps) and some reliance on graphical intuition instead of proof marginally reduce precision, but the overall application remains consistently relevant and effective.

The essayâs title, abstract, introduction and conclusion work together to communicate the topicâapplying Pellâtype equations and Vieta jumping to an OSN divisibility problemâclearly and coherently. The context of the Olympiad question and the mathematical significance of the two techniques are explained early, and section headings guide the reader through the argument. Minor language awkwardness and isolated digressions (e.g. lengthy textbook-style expositions) prevent a flawless, highly concise presentation, so a solid but not perfect score in the top band is appropriate.

The essay follows a conventional EE structure: title page, abstract, contents, sequentially numbered sections, conclusion and bibliography.  The ordering supports comprehension and the reader can trace the line of argument with ease.  Minor issuesâoverly long subsections, occasional digressions and a contents page lacking dynamic page referencesâprevent a flawless rating but the structure clearly aids understanding, deserving the top band.

The investigation is grounded in seven authoritative texts and articles, each explicitly cited where ideas are used.  Sources are clearly relevant to the research question, covering Pell theory, Vieta jumping and modular arithmetic.  The student integrates these materials into the argument rather than merely summarising them, demonstrating judicious and mostly critical selection.  A slightly greater diversity (e.g. primary research papers) or deeper cross-comparison would lift the work to the highest level, so a strong score within the 7-9 band is merited.

The student offers a reflective comparison between the chosen techniques and a lemma-based alternative, noting efficiency, rigour and competition suitability.  While this demonstrates emerging evaluative thinking, the discussion remains somewhat general and does not probe the limitations of each approach or possible sources of error in depth.  Consequently, the argument is reasoned and partially critical but does not attain the sophistication required for the top band.

The student selects two complementary analytical methods, justifies their choice, and applies a case-by-case strategy that is well suited to the question.  Seven varied, reputable sources inform the mathematical background.  Evidence of effective planning is clear in the stepwise treatment of gcd cases, Pell reductions and Vieta jumps.  Nonetheless, several derivations are compressed and some graph-based demonstrations are more illustrative than rigorous, indicating that the methodology, though complete, is not exemplary.

annotations.comment: Formatting: Page numbers are included consistently, fulfilling the IB requirement for pagination.
Presentation: The clear â1. Introductionâ heading establishes a familiar academic structure, guiding the reader effectively.
Strength: The congruence notation $$a quiv r	ext{ pmod{n}}$$ is correctly introduced and applied.
Presentation: The detailed table of contents with numbered sections supports a clear navigational structure for the reader.
Consider adding dynamic hyperlinks or PDF bookmarks in the table of contents to improve navigation, especially for lengthy subsections.
Strength: The IMO example is well chosen, demonstrating the classical application of standard Vieta jumping to a known competition problem.
Strength: The section on integer divisibility effectively introduces modular arithmetic as essential background for the main problem.
Formatting: A precise word count is provided on the title page, satisfying the IB requirement for extended essays.
Strength: The Olympiad problem is stated precisely in mathematical terms, demonstrating subjectâspecific knowledge and framing the investigation clearly.
Background on Vieta jumping appears before a formal definition; consider moving the general definition here to streamline the narrative flow.
Strength: The title clearly communicates the scope and focus on Pellâtype equations and Vieta jumping, immediately orienting the reader to the mathematical investigation.
Strength: The research question is explicitly stated, focused on a single Olympiad problem and two methods, and remains central throughout the essay.
The personal anecdote opening is engaging but could be condensed; it uses substantial word count without advancing the mathematical argument.
Pellâs equation $$x^2 - dy^2 = 1$$ is correctly stated, but explicitly noting that $$d$$ must be nonsquare would improve mathematical precision.
Strength: The generalization to the Pellâtype equation $$x^2 - dy^2 = a$$ is clearly introduced, showing good command of the concept.
The list of key properties is helpful but inconsistent in numbering; adopt a uniform list style to enhance presentation clarity.
The continued fraction expansion $$[1; ar2]=3/2$$ is correct, but a brief note on how periodicity yields the fundamental solution would deepen the exposition.
Graph 1 is informative but the caption omits axis labels; include explicit x- and y-axis annotations to meet formal IB presentation standards.
Strength: The connection back to the essayâs methodology clarifies why the Pell example is directly relevant to solving the research problem.
The Vieta jumping definition is described in prose but would benefit from a concise mathematical statement of the rootâflipping operation to reinforce understanding.
The example solution truncates before completing the contradiction; explicitly show the minimality argument to fully validate the Vieta jumping step.
In combining divisibility conditions to obtain $$pn mid	ext{ }n^2+p^2+4$$, explicitly cite the theorem that permits adding divisibilities for clarity.
Strength: The concise derivation of $$pn mid	ext{ }n^2+p^2+4$$ demonstrates critical application of divisibility properties.
Strength: The primeâconstraint case is well handled by showing $$p	ext{ mid }n^2+4ightarrow p=2$$, efficiently narrowing possibilities.
In the step list for standard Vieta jumping, Step 2 is vague; specify how to recast the given relationship as a quadratic in one variable for clarity and rigor.
Strength: The Methodology section clearly signals a transition to solving the OSN problem, maintaining focus on the research question.
While the divisibility proof is correct, hyperlinking or in-text referencing of Khurmiâs source here would improve attribution and traceability.
Consider adding a brief roadmap at the start of the case analysis to preview why gcd cases are split, strengthening structural clarity.
